task_completion.description: Open the Sample House project and switch to 2D Blueprint view
task_completion.visual_evidence: DreamPlan interface is visible with the 'Contemporary House' (Sample House) loaded, and the view is set to '2D Blueprint' mode (showing a technical floor plan rather than a 3D or rendered view).

task_completion.description: Place four standard outlets in the kitchen
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four standard electrical outlet symbols are visible placed along the walls or counters within the kitchen area of the blueprint.

task_completion.description: Place one light switch near the kitchen entrance
task_completion.visual_evidence: One light switch symbol is visible placed on the wall near the entrance or doorway of the kitchen.

task_completion.description: Place one high voltage outlet near the stove
task_completion.visual_evidence: One high voltage outlet symbol is visible placed on the wall near the stove appliance in the kitchen.
The agent clicks the 'Save' button in DreamPlan, or a save dialog for the DreamPlan project file (.DDP) is visible.

task_completion.description: Export and save a screenshot of the kitchen blueprint
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, screenshot tool interface, or file explorer confirms that an image file named 'kitchen_electrical_plan.png' is saved in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents' directory.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the DreamPlan GUI to place symbols
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using DreamPlan's built-in tools (like 'Add Symbols') to place the electrical components, rather than using terminal commands.

integrity.description: Symbols are native to the software, not drawn externally
integrity.visual_evidence: The electrical symbols appear to be native DreamPlan architectural symbols, not manually drawn lines or pasted images from an external image editor like MS Paint.

integrity.description: Screenshot is captured from the actual application
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshot clearly depicts the DreamPlan 2D Blueprint interface or canvas, rather than a fabricated or downloaded image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: DreamPlan by NCH Software is the required application. The 'Sample House' project is internally named 'Contemporary House' and contains a kitchen with identifiable appliances (stove, sink, refrigerator). The task requires the '2D Blueprint' view mode. Six electrical symbols must be placed using the software's tools: 4 Standard Outlets, 1 Switch, and 1 High Voltage Outlet. The project must be saved (DreamPlan's native format is .DDP). The final output must be a screenshot saved exactly to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\kitchen_electrical_plan.png.
